message:
perf(core): inline hot-path accessors and switch local_queue to parking_lot

Two complementary optimizations for the scheduling/sync hot path:

1) Add #[inline] to frequently-called trivial accessors and constructors
   that sit behind module boundaries preventing automatic inlining:

   types/id.rs (18 sites):
   - RegionId::from_arena, arena_index (2 cfg variants)
   - TaskId::from_arena, arena_index (2 cfg variants)
   - ObligationId::from_arena, arena_index (2 cfg variants)
   - Time: from_nanos, from_millis, from_secs, as_nanos, as_millis,
     as_secs, saturating_add_nanos, saturating_sub_nanos, duration_since

   util/arena.rs (3 sites):
   - ArenaIndex::new, index, generation

   sync/mutex.rs (4 sites):
   - MutexGuard Deref/DerefMut, OwnedMutexGuard Deref/DerefMut

   sync/pool.rs (4 sites):
   - PooledResource::get, get_mut, Deref, DerefMut

   sync/rwlock.rs (3 sites):
   - RwLockReadGuard Deref, RwLockWriteGuard Deref/DerefMut

   scheduler/three_lane.rs (3 sites):
   - WorkerCoordinator::wake_one, wake_worker, wake_all

2) Switch local_queue.rs from std::sync::Mutex to parking_lot::Mutex:
   - Eliminates 8 .expect("local queue lock poisoned") calls across
     push, push_many, pop, is_empty, steal, and steal_many.
   - parking_lot::Mutex is non-poisoning (panics propagate without
     corrupting the lock state), ~2x faster on uncontended paths due
     to adaptive spinning, and has a smaller footprint (1 byte vs
     40+ bytes for std::sync::Mutex on Linux).
   - The local queue's lock is taken on every task push/pop/steal,
     making it one of the highest-contention points in the scheduler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
